title: Lobelia brevisepala
taxonomy: Lobelia brevisepala is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It belongs to the genus Lobelia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as lobelias or bluebells. The family Campanulaceae is a large group of flowering plants that also includes bellflowers and harebells. Lobelia brevisepala is native to certain regions, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Campanulaceae family. The species is further classified using binomial nomenclature, with its full classification being Lobelia brevisepala.
uses: Lobelia brevisepala is a plant species that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Africa. Its medicinal uses include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties.
